cards.name: The Balance
cards.rules_text:
Equilibrium — Each player's life total becomes equal to the highest life total among all players.
Parity — Each player sacrifices creatures, planeswalkers, artifacts, and enchantments until they control the same number of them as the player who controls the fewest.
Reckoning — Each player discards cards until their hand size is equal to the lowest hand size among all players.
Erosion — Each player sacrifices lands until they control the same number of lands as the player who controls the fewest.
cards.flavor_text: What took a lifetime to build, fate undoes in a breath.

cards.name: The Comet
cards.rules_text:
Suspend 2 — {0}{U}{U} (When you draw The Comet from the Deck of Many Things, instead of casting it immediately, exile it with two time counters on it. At the beginning of your upkeep, remove a time counter. When the last is removed, cast it without paying its mana cost.)
Impact — When you cast The Comet, you may choose target creature you control. If you do, all other creatures phase out until end of turn.
Until end of turn, the chosen creature gains flying, double strike, and "Whenever this creature deals combat damage to a player, draw two cards."
At the beginning of your end step, if a creature was chosen this way and that creature didn't deal combat damage to a player this turn, exile it.
cards.flavor_text: It falls not to destroy, but to anoint.

cards.name: The Donjon
cards.rules_text:
Vanishing — When you draw this card, you become dimensionally displaced. Create a Temporal Stasis Sphere emblem and exile all permanents you control until that emblem leaves.
Eternal Damnation — You can't draw from the Deck of Many Things for the remainder of the game.
cards.flavor_text: The cruelest prison has no walls—only the endless moment between tick and tock.

cards.name: Curse of Weakness
cards.rules_text:
Enchant player
• If a source would deal damage to you or a permanent you control, it deals that much plus 2 damage instead.
• Spells your opponents cast that target you cost {2} less.
• At the beginning of your upkeep, you lose 2 life and sacrifice a permanent unless you discard a card.
• Your maximum hand size is reduced by 3.
Eternal Gaze — The Euryale can't be removed in any way except by an effect from a card named The Fates.
cards.flavor_text: Her gaze finds you still, even behind closed eyes.

cards.name: The Fates
cards.rules_text:
The Fates can't be removed from the battlefield by spells or abilities your opponents control.
{0}, Shuffle The Fates into the Deck of Many Things: Choose one —
• Rewind — Remove all effects, counters, and modifications applied by a card drawn from the Deck of Many Things. Return affected permanents and players to their state before that card was drawn.
• Deny — Choose a spell, permanent, or card drawn from the Deck of Many Things. Exile it.
• Cleanse — Exile target emblem or curse card named Curse of Weakness.
• Weave — Look at the top three cards of the Deck of Many Things and put them back in any order. Then, you may draw a card from the Deck of Many Things.
cards.flavor_text: What was written can be unwritten. What was done can be undone. But only once.

cards.name: Vengeful Archfiend
cards.rules_text:
Suspend X — {0}{B}{R} (X is the result of rolling a d4. Exile this with that many time counters. At the beginning of your upkeep, remove one. When the last is removed, cast it.)
Vendetta — When Vengeful Archfiend enters, a random opponent gains control of it, and you become its nemesis.
Haste, trample, menace
Vengeful Archfiend can't block and attacks its nemesis each combat if able.
Torment — Whenever Vengeful Archfiend deals combat damage to its nemesis, put a rage counter on it. That player discards a card and sacrifices a permanent.
Vengeful Archfiend gets +2/+0 for each rage counter on it.
When Vengeful Archfiend leaves or its nemesis loses the game, shuffle it into the Deck of Many Things.
cards.flavor_text: "Your soul bears my mark. Now suffer eternal persecution."

cards.name: The Fool
cards.rules_text:
Misfortune — Sacrifice two nontoken permanents, discard two cards, skip your next draw step, mill ten cards, and lose 5 life.
Draw a card from the Deck of Many Things.
Exile The Fool.
cards.flavor_text: The Fool stumbles not into emptiness, but into fate itself.

cards.name: The Gem
cards.rules_text:
Fortune — When Gem enters, create X Treasure tokens, where X is the number of lands on the battlefield.
Opulence — At the beginning of your upkeep, create two Treasure tokens.
Liquidate — {T}, Sacrifice Gem: Create ten Treasure tokens.
cards.flavor_text: Count it if you like. There will always be more.

cards.name: The Jester
cards.rules_text:
Mirth — Draw three cards, scry 3, you may play an additional land this turn, create a Treasure token, and gain 5 life. (Treasure tokens are artifacts with "{T}, Sacrifice this artifact: Add one mana of any color.")
You may draw up to two additional cards from the Deck of Many Things.
Exile The Jester.
cards.flavor_text: The only honest fool is the one who knows the punchline before you do.

cards.name: The Key
cards.rules_text:
Hexproof
Living weapon (When this enters, create a 0/0 black Phyrexian Germ token, then attach this to it.)
Enhancement — At the beginning of your upkeep or whenever equipped creature deals combat damage to a player, put a power counter on The Key.
Equipped creature gets +2/+0 for each power counter on The Key.
Transmutation — {0}: Choose two — Haste, flying, fear, skulk, menace, intimidate, double strike, trample, bushido 3, flanking, battle cry, provoke, exalted, afflict 3, frenzy 3, deathtouch, toxic 2, prowess, infect, wither, or annihilator 1. Equipped creature loses abilities granted this way, then gains the chosen abilities until end of turn.
Equip {0}
cards.flavor_text: It was a sword when she drew it, and a lance when she threw it, and an axe when the final blow fell.

cards.name: The Knight
cards.rules_text:
Double strike, menace
Oath — Other Knights you control get +1/+1 and have vigilance.
Conscription — When Knight enters or attacks, create two 2/2 white and black Knight tokens.
Dark Blessing — Pay {X} life, where X is the number of Knights you control: Put that many +1/+1 counters on Knights you control, divided as you choose, and each of those creatures gain lifelink until end of turn. Activate this ability anytime you could cast a sorcery.
cards.flavor_text: "Death refused my oath. Now I serve through eternal undeath."

cards.name: The Moon
cards.rules_text:
The Moon enters with three wish counters.
Lunar Wish — {T}, Remove a wish counter: Choose one —
• Search your library for a card, put it into your hand, then shuffle.
• Target player gains 10 life and draws three cards.
• Cast a spell from your hand without paying its mana cost.
• Exile target nonland permanent.
• Create a 7/7 colorless Spirit token with flying and vigilance.
• Return target permanent card from your graveyard to the battlefield.
• Return target permanent card you own from exile to the battlefield.
Whenever The Moon becomes tapped, scry 1.
When The Moon has no wish counters, sacrifice it.
cards.flavor_text: Your heart's desire manifests in the silvery moonlight.

cards.name: The Puzzle
cards.rules_text:
Tempt Fate — When The Puzzle enters, you may draw an additional card from the Deck of Many Things.
Enchant player
Enchanted player plays with their hand revealed and skips their draw step.
Confusion — At the beginning of enchanted player's upkeep, that player exiles a card from their hand face down. They may look at and play that card as if it were in their hand.
Ineptitude — Whenever enchanted player casts a spell, if it wasn't cast from exile with The Puzzle, counter it.
Consolation — At the beginning of enchanted player's end step, they draw a card.
cards.flavor_text: He solved a thousand riddles in his youth. Now he cannot recall his own name.

cards.name: The Rogue
cards.rules_text:
Haste, first strike, deathtouch, ward {2}
Skulk (This creature can't be blocked by creatures with greater power.)
Subversion — When The Rogue enters, gain control of target creature an opponent controls with the highest power. Untap it. It gains haste until end of turn.
Backstab — If The Rogue would deal combat damage to a player and it wasn't blocked, it deals three times that much damage to that player instead. When it deals combat damage to a player this way, that player discards a card. You may cast target instant or sorcery card from that player's graveyard this turn without paying its mana cost.
Betrayal — When The Rogue leaves, an opponent at random gains control of target creature you control with the lowest power.
cards.flavor_text: Trust is a currency best spent quickly.

cards.name: The Ruin
cards.rules_text:
Bankruptcy — Sacrifice all artifacts you control, then exile all artifact cards from your hand and graveyard.
Foreclosure — Sacrifice all lands you control. For the next three turns, you can't play lands.
Auction — Starting with the player to your left and proceeding in turn order, each opponent chooses a non-artifact, non-land permanent you control and gains control of it. Repeat this process until you control no non-artifact, non-land permanents.
cards.flavor_text: The vaults stand open. The vaults stand empty.

cards.name: The Vizier
cards.rules_text:
When The Vizier enters, exile the top seven cards of your library face down. You may look at them at any time.
Meditation — {0}, {T}: Exile the top three cards of your library face down. Look at all cards exiled with The Vizier. Put one into your hand. You may cast it this turn as though it had flash. If you do and it's an instant or sorcery spell, copy it. You may choose new targets for the copy.
Epiphany — At the beginning of your upkeep, put a wisdom counter on The Vizier. If it has two or more, you may search your library for a card and exile it. You may cast that card from exile without paying its mana cost for the rest of the game.
Guidance — Whenever you cast a spell that shares a color with a card exiled with The Vizier, scry 2 and draw a card. That spell costs {1} less. If it was cast from exile and it's an instant or sorcery spell, copy it. You may choose new targets for the copy.
cards.flavor_text: "In each moment lies a thousand paths. I have glimpsed them all."

cards.name: Avatar of Death
cards.rules_text:
Flying, indestructible, menace, deathtouch, wither
Protection from colored spells
When Avatar of Death enters, mark the player who summoned it. Each player's life total becomes 10. A random opponent gains control of Avatar of Death.
Avatar of Death attacks the marked player each combat if able and can't block.
When Avatar of Death dies or the marked player loses the game, shuffle it into the Deck of Many Things.
Whenever the marked player or Avatar of Death is targeted or dealt damage by an opponent, that opponent creates a token copy of Avatar of Death.
Creatures dealt lethal damage by Avatar of Death are exiled. When Avatar of Death deals combat damage to a player, that player loses the game.
cards.flavor_text: "You mistake my patience for mercy." — Death

cards.name: The Star
cards.rules_text:
Transcendence — Roll two d8. If both results are the same, reroll. You get an enhancement emblem for each number rolled:
1. Agility: "Creatures you control get +2/+2 and have double strike and vigilance."
2. Charisma: "Whenever a creature enters under your control, create a 1/1 white Soldier token."
3. Focus: "You may cast spells as though they had flash. They cost {2} less."
4. Fortitude: "If your life total is less than 50, it becomes 50. You have hexproof. At the beginning of your upkeep, gain 3 life."
5. Intellect: "At the beginning of your draw step, draw two additional cards."
6. Wisdom: "You have no maximum hand size. You may play two additional lands each turn."
7. Wit: "The first spell you cast each turn can't be countered and has cascade."
8. Cunning: "Whenever an opponent casts their first spell each turn, draw a card."
cards.flavor_text: It did not make her stronger. It reminded her she had always been limitless.

cards.name: The Sun
cards.rules_text:
Radiance — Draw seven cards, scry 5, you may play up to two additional lands this turn, you have no maximum hand size for the rest of the game, gain 20 life, and create three Treasure tokens. (They're artifacts with "{T}, Sacrifice this artifact: Add one mana of any color.")
Illumination — Search target player's library for an artifact card and put it onto the battlefield under your control. Then that player shuffles their library.
cards.flavor_text: The last thing you see before enlightenment is everything you were missing.

cards.name: The Talons
cards.rules_text:
Disintegration — Destroy all Equipment you control.
Vanishing — Exile all non-Equipment artifacts you control.
cards.flavor_text: Every relic, every charm, every ring -- gone between one heartbeat and the next.

cards.name: The Throne
cards.rules_text:
Rightful Claim — When The Throne enters, search your library for up to two land cards, put them onto the battlefield tapped, then shuffle. Create X 2/2 white Knight tokens with vigilance, where X is the number of lands you control. Each opponent creates 2X 2/2 black Zombie tokens. Zombie tokens created by Rightful Claim attack each combat if able.
Throne's Authority — Other non-Soldier creatures you control get +1/+1 and have "When this creature dies, create a 1/1 white Soldier token."
Royal Influence — {0}, {T}: Gain control of target creature an opponent controls for as long as you control The Throne. Untap it. It gains haste and "Whenever this creature deals combat damage to a player, create two 1/1 white Soldier tokens." Activate only during your turn.
cards.flavor_text: The throne was empty when she arrived. It will never be empty again.

cards.name: The Void
cards.rules_text:
Soul Imprisonment — When you draw this card, exile your soul and you become incapacitated. Create a colorless Soul Prison artifact token.
Eternal Damnation — You can't draw from the Deck of Many Things for the remainder of the game.
cards.flavor_text: The soul knows its jailer, but the body knows only emptiness.

cards.name: Deck of Many Things
cards.rules_text:
The Deck of Many Things is a shared deck in the command zone.
DRAW AT YOUR OWN RISK!
Setup —
• Shuffle the Deck and place it in the command zone.
• Each player begins the game with 1 Fate Point (maximum 1).
Drawing —
Beginning on your fifth turn, once during your precombat main phase, you may spend a Fate Point to draw a card from the Deck.
Gaining Fate Points —
If you have no Fate Points, you may roll a d6 during your main phase. On a 6, you gain 1 Fate Point. If you already have one, you may draw a card from the Deck instead.
Resolution —
Cards drawn from the Deck are cast immediately without paying their mana cost. Drawing from the Deck can't be countered, doesn't use the stack, and can only be responded to by The Fates.
• Cards that would leave the battlefield are shuffled back into the Deck, except The Jester and The Fool, which are exiled.
• Resolve drawn cards one at a time in the order drawn.
• Emblems from the Deck can't be removed except by effects that specifically remove emblems.
• The legend rule doesn't apply to permanents from the Deck.
Optional Rules —
• Each player may draw one card from the Deck at the start of the game without spending a Fate Point.
• When drawing, you may declare 1 to 4 cards instead of one. Resolve each in order.
cards.flavor_text: "Sometimes the cards you're dealt become the fate you're given."

cards.name: Soul Guardian
cards.rules_text:
Defender, flying, hexproof
Whenever Soul Guardian blocks a creature, that creature gets -2/-2 until end of turn.
Whenever a creature blocked by Soul Guardian dies during combat, that creature's controller discards a card.
cards.flavor_text: It does not guard the prison. It guards what remains of you.

cards.name: Soul Prison
cards.rules_text:
When Soul Prison enters, put a despair counter on it and create two 6/8 colorless Guardian Horror Spirit tokens named Soul Guardian with defender, flying, and hexproof.
You get an emblem with "You are incapacitated. You can't gain life, attack with creatures, or activate nonmana abilities. You may cast only one spell each turn."
At the beginning of your upkeep, put a despair counter on Soul Prison. Spells you cast cost {1} more for each despair counter on Soul Prison.
Sacrifice fifteen or more power of creatures you control: Sacrifice Soul Prison and remove your incapacitated emblem.
When Soul Prison leaves, remove your incapacitated emblem.
cards.flavor_text: A fate worse than death—to watch eternity pass by, with memories slipping away.

cards.name: Temporal Stasis Sphere
cards.rules_text:
Tick Tock — When Temporal Stasis Sphere enters, roll a d4 and add 3 to the result. Put that many time counters on it. At the beginning of your upkeep, remove a time counter and mill three cards. When the last time counter is removed, exile Temporal Stasis Sphere at the beginning of the next end step.
You are dimensionally displaced. (A dimensionally displaced player can't be affected by any effect in the main game. They remain in the main game's turn order but can't interact with or be interacted with by other players. Spells you cast or permanents you play are exiled. Starting with your next turn, skip your draw step on even-numbered turns. Your maximum hand size is reduced by 2.)
When Temporal Stasis Sphere leaves, return and cast all cards exiled with it and The Donjon onto the battlefield at the beginning of your next upkeep. For each card returned this way, lose 1 life.

cards.name: The Aberration
cards.rules_text:
Living weapon (When this Equipment enters, create a 0/0 black Phyrexian Germ token, then attach this to it.)
Equipped creature gets +2/+2 and has flying and menace.
At the beginning of your upkeep, you may exile the top card of target opponent's library face down. You may look at and cast cards exiled with The Aberration, spending mana as though it were any color.
Whenever equipped creature deals combat damage to a player, choose one —
• Look at the top X cards of that player's library, where X is the damage dealt. Put one into your hand and the rest into their graveyard.
• That player reveals their hand. You may cast a spell from it without paying its mana cost.
Equip {0}
cards.flavor_text: The voices whisper secrets you were never meant to know. They never stop.

cards.name: The Beast
cards.rules_text:
When The Beast enters, roll 2d12. Put that many time counters on it and create that many 2/2 green Beast tokens.
At the beginning of your upkeep, remove a time counter. When the last counter is removed, sacrifice The Beast.
Creatures you control are green Beasts in addition to their other colors and types and have base power and toughness at least 2/2.
At the beginning of combat on your turn, roll 2d6. Creatures you control have base power and toughness equal to the result until end of turn.
Feral Rage — {0}, {T}: Until end of turn, creatures you control gain double strike and trample.
cards.flavor_text: Nature cares not for what you were—only what you become.

cards.name: The Book
cards.rules_text:
When The Book enters, roll a d6 and add 2. The result is the number of experience counters you get.
{T}: Choose one —
• Enlighten — Exile a card from your hand: Put a page counter on The Book. For each page counter on The Book, you may play an additional land on each of your turns and draw an additional card during each of your draw steps.
• Comprehend — Look at the top X cards of your library, where X is the number of experience counters you have. You may cast one spell from among them without paying its mana cost if it shares a type with a card exiled with The Book. Put the rest on the bottom of your library in any order.
cards.flavor_text: Every page you turn reveals a language you didn't know you spoke.

cards.name: The Bridge
cards.rules_text:
The Bridge enters with a number of portal counters on it equal to 2 plus the result of rolling a d12.
{T}, Remove a portal counter: Exile target creature you control, then return it to the battlefield under your control. You may have it enter the battlefield as a copy of another creature on the battlefield until end of turn.
When The Bridge has no portal counters on it, sacrifice it.
cards.flavor_text: Step through. You'll arrive before you know where you're going.

cards.name: The Campfire
cards.rules_text:
When The Campfire enters, choose three —
• Return target permanent card from your graveyard to the battlefield.
• Put three +1/+1 counters on each creature you control.
• Draw four cards and gain 10 life.
• Remove all counters from target permanent.
• Create four 1/1 white Human creature tokens.
At the beginning of your upkeep, gain 2 life and scry 1.
cards.flavor_text: Sit. Rest. Remember who you were before the fighting began.

cards.name: The Cavern
cards.rules_text:
{T}: Add {G}.
The Cavern enters with a number of depth counters on it equal to the result of rolling a d12.
Blindsight — As long as The Cavern has a depth counter on it, creatures you control have first strike and reach.
At the beginning of your upkeep, remove a depth counter from The Cavern.
{1}, {T}: Target creature you control gains hexproof until end of turn.
cards.flavor_text: In the deepest dark, the blind have the advantage.

cards.name: The Celestial
cards.rules_text:
Enchant creature
Living Aura (When this enters, if you control no creatures, create a 0/0 Spirit token, then attach this to it.)
Enchanted creature gets +4/+4, has flying and vigilance, is an Angel in addition to its other types, and has "Whenever this deals combat damage to a player, exile target nonland permanent that player controls until The Celestial leaves."
At the beginning of your upkeep, create two 4/4 white Angel tokens with flying and vigilance.
Whenever enchanted creature attacks, choose one —
• Angels you control gain lifelink and double strike until end of turn.
• Angels you control gain indestructible and "Whenever this deals combat damage to a player, draw a card" until end of turn.
cards.flavor_text: She knelt a mortal. She rose radiant.

cards.name: The Construct
cards.rules_text:
Propagate — When The Construct enters, put X 0/1 blue and black Homunculus artifact tokens onto the battlefield, where X is the number of artifacts you control.
Whenever a Homunculus enters under your control, each opponent loses 1 life. Then, if you control three or more Homunculi, draw a card.
{T}, Sacrifice a Homunculus: Choose one — Activate only as a sorcery.
• Replicate — Create a non-legendary token copy of The Construct.
• Dominate — Gain control of target creature with power less than or equal to the number of Homunculi you control until end of turn. Untap it. It becomes a Homunculus in addition to its other types and gains haste.
cards.flavor_text: One became two. Two became many. Many became legion.

cards.name: Curse of Death's Door
cards.rules_text:
Enchant player
When Curse of Death's Door enters, enchanted player's life total becomes 0.
Enchanted player can't lose the game for having 0 or less life and can't gain life except from this curse.
At the beginning of enchanted player's upkeep, roll a d20:
• 1 | Two death counters.
• 2–9 | One death counter.
• 10–19 | One life counter.
• 20 | Gain 1 life, then sacrifice Curse of Death's Door.
Whenever enchanted player is dealt damage, they get a death counter.
When enchanted player has three or more death counters, they lose the game.
When enchanted player has three or more life counters, they gain 1 life and sacrifice Curse of Death's Door.
cards.flavor_text: Some corpses refuse to let go of life. Others refuse to let life go.

cards.name: The Crossroads
cards.rules_text:
Blink Step — Roll a d12 and put that many blink counters on you.
You get an emblem with "At the beginning of combat on your turn, you may remove a blink counter from yourself. If you do, exile target creature you control, then return it to the battlefield under your control. It gains haste until end of turn."
cards.flavor_text: The air shimmers where you stood, and you're already somewhere else.

cards.name: The Door
cards.rules_text:
When The Door enters, if it isn't a token, put eight portal counters on it.
Portal — {T}, Remove a portal counter: Choose one —
• Search your library for a land and put it onto the battlefield tapped. Return it to your hand at the next end step unless you remove a portal counter.
• Exile target permanent other than The Door. Return it under its owner's control at the next end step unless you remove a portal counter.
• Put a permanent card with mana value 6 or less from your hand onto the battlefield. Return it to your hand at the next end step unless you remove a portal counter.
• Create a token copy of target permanent. It gains "When The Door leaves, sacrifice this" unless you remove a portal counter.
When The Door has no portal counters, sacrifice it.
cards.flavor_text: Eight passages. Eight possibilities. Choose wisely -- each one closes behind you.

cards.name: The Dragon
cards.rules_text:
Flying, menace
The Dragon gains abilities based on colors of other Dragons you control:
• White: Vigilance, protection from black. Other Dragons get +2/+2 and vigilance.
• Blue: Ward {3}, hexproof. Other Dragons have ward {3} and can't be countered.
• Black: Deathtouch, lifelink. Other Dragons have deathtouch and "Whenever this deals damage, each opponent loses 1 life."
• Red: Double strike. Other Dragons have first strike and "When this attacks, it deals 2 damage to any target."
• Green: Trample, reach. Other Dragons get +3/+1 and trample.
Whenever The Dragon attacks, create two 3/3 Dragon tokens with flying of a random color. They gain that color's abilities.
Growth — {T}, Sacrifice a Dragon: Put three +1/+1 counters on The Dragon and draw a card.
cards.flavor_text: Fate is not a line, but a tapestry—and I am every thread.

cards.name: The Elemental
cards.rules_text:
When The Elemental enters, choose a color.
You and permanents you control gain protection from the chosen color. (They can't be dealt damage, enchanted, blocked, or targeted by anything of the chosen color.)
{T}: Add three mana of the chosen color.
Spells you cast of the chosen color cost {1} less.
Whenever you cast a spell of the chosen color, you may change the chosen color.
cards.flavor_text: Choose your element. Become its vessel. Let all else break against you.

cards.name: The Expert
cards.rules_text:
(Gain the next level as a sorcery to add its ability.)
If damage would be dealt to you or a creature you control, prevent X of that damage. Creatures you control have bushido X, where X is The Expert's level.
Whenever one or more creatures you control deal combat damage to a player, draw a card.
Level 2 — {2}: Dexterity
Creatures you control have first strike, haste, and flanking.
Damage dealt by creatures you control can't be prevented.
Level 3 — {3}: Dexterity
Creature spells you cast have flash.
Creatures you control have flying, hexproof, and "If this creature would be dealt damage, you may have it phase out instead."
Level 4 — {4}: Dexterity
Creatures you control have skulk and double strike.
Whenever a creature you control deals combat damage to a creature, you may have it deal that much damage to another target creature that creature's controller controls.

cards.name: The Fey
cards.rules_text:
Shroud, vanishing 10
Shimmer — When The Fey enters, exile your hand and library. Create X 0/1 green and blue Faerie tokens with flying, where X is the number of cards exiled this way.
If you would draw a card while your library has no cards in it, skip that draw instead.
Whenever a Faerie you control leaves, look at three cards at random exiled with The Fey. You may cast one of them this turn without paying its mana cost.
{T}, Sacrifice a Faerie: Exile target nonland permanent. Its controller reveals cards from the top of their library until they reveal a nonland permanent and puts it onto the battlefield. Put the rest on the bottom in a random order.
When The Fey leaves, shuffle all cards exiled with it into their owner's library.
cards.flavor_text: Time bends like a willow in the Feywild, and every moment is a gamble with eternity.

cards.name: The Fiend
cards.rules_text:
Flash, flying
When The Fiend enters, starting with you in turn order, each player may wager. Each player who declines for the first time discards a card, loses 5 life, and mills three. Each player may wager only up to three times. Repeat this process until all players decline.
To wager, choose a mode not yet chosen by you this turn and flip a coin:
• Power — Win: Put six +1/+1 counters on each creature you control. Lose: Sacrifice six creatures.
• Knowledge — Win: Draw six cards. Lose: Mill six cards.
• Wealth — Win: Search your library for six lands, put them onto the battlefield tapped, then shuffle. Lose: Sacrifice six lands.
• Vengeance — Win: Target player sacrifices six permanents. Lose: You sacrifice six permanents.
• Immortality — Win: Gain 66 life. Lose: Your life total becomes 6.
• Dominion — Win: Gain control of six permanents target player controls. Lose: Target opponent gains control of six permanents you control.
When The Fiend becomes the target of a spell or ability, is dealt damage, or all players declined to wager this turn, shuffle it into its owner's library.
cards.flavor_text: Pick your poison carefully, mortal. The greater the reward, the greater the fall.

cards.name: The Giant
cards.rules_text:
When The Giant enters, you gain 20 life.
Each creature you control has base power and toughness at least 10/10, trample, reach, and "This creature can block up to three creatures each combat and deals excess combat damage to attacking player while blocking."
If a source an opponent controls would deal damage to you or permanents you control, prevent 1 of that damage.
cards.flavor_text: "The good news is we can see the objective. The bad news is it can reach us too." —Scout's last report

cards.name: The Humanoid
cards.rules_text:
Create a legendary 5/5 white Human Soldier creature token named Loyal Companion with vigilance, lifelink, and indestructible.
Loyal Companion has "Whenever you are dealt damage by a source an opponent controls, Loyal Companion deals that much damage to that source's controller."
When you lose the game or are eliminated, exile Loyal Companion.
cards.flavor_text: It breathed its first breath when you breathed your last prayer.

cards.name: The Juggernaut
cards.rules_text:
(Gain the next level as a sorcery to add its ability.)
Creatures you control get +0/+X, absorb X, and ward {X}, where X is The Juggernaut's level.
Whenever one or more creatures you control are dealt damage and survive, draw a card.
Level 2 — {2}: Constitution
Creatures you control have vigilance and enter with a shield counter.
Your opponents can't reduce your creatures' toughness or cause you to sacrifice permanents.
Level 3 — {3}: Constitution
Creatures you control have persist.
Whenever a creature you control is dealt damage and survives, put that many +1/+1 counters on it.
Level 4 — {4}: Constitution
Creatures you control have indestructible and "Whenever this creature attacks, defending player sacrifices a nonland permanent."

cards.name: The Lance
cards.rules_text:
(Gain the next level as a sorcery to add its ability.)
At the beginning of your upkeep, draw a card, gain X life, and create X 1/1 Knight tokens with vigilance, where X is The Lance's level.
Level 2 — {2}: Attributes
Creatures you control have exalted and mentor.
At the beginning of your upkeep, put a +1/+1 counter on each creature you control.
Level 3 — {3}: Attributes
Whenever you attack, creatures you control get +X/+X until end of turn, where X is the number of attacking creatures.
At the beginning of your end step, proliferate.
Level 4 — {4}: Attributes
Whenever you attack for the first time each turn, untap all creatures you control. After this phase, there is an additional combat phase.
Whenever you proliferate, proliferate again.

cards.name: The Mage
cards.rules_text:
(Gain the next level as a sorcery to add its ability.)
At the beginning of your upkeep, scry X, then draw a card. The first spell you cast each turn costs {X} less, where X is The Mage's level.
Level 2 — {2}: Intelligence
Spells you cast can't be countered and have convoke. Creatures you control have prowess.
The first instant or sorcery you cast each turn has "When you cast this spell, copy it. You may choose new targets for the copy."
Level 3 — {3}: Intelligence
Spells you cast have cascade.
You may cast instant and sorcery spells from your graveyard.
Level 4 — {4}: Intelligence
Spells you cast have storm.
Whenever you cast an instant or sorcery, copy it. You may choose new targets for the copy.
You have hexproof.

cards.name: The Map
cards.rules_text:
When The Map enters, put three seek counters on it.
{T}, Remove a seek counter: Choose one —
• Locate — Name a card. Reveal cards from the top of target player's library until you reveal it. Put it into your hand and shuffle the rest into that library. That player loses life equal to its mana value.
• Plunder — Look at target player's hand and choose a nonland card. That player discards it. You may cast it this turn, spending mana as though it were any color. If you don't, exile it.
• Cartograph — Search your library for an artifact, enchantment, or planeswalker card with mana value 4 or less and put it onto the battlefield. If it's a planeswalker, it enters with an additional loyalty counter. Then shuffle.
cards.flavor_text: X marks the spot. Every spot.

cards.name: Curse of Exhaustion
cards.rules_text:
Enchant player
When Curse of Exhaustion enters, roll a d3. Put that many exhaustion counters on enchanted player.
For each exhaustion counter on enchanted player:
0 — Sacrifice Curse of Exhaustion.
1 — Skip your draw step unless you discard a card.
2+ Creatures you control get -2/-2.
3+ Spells you cast cost {1} more and abilities you activate cost {1} more to activate.
4+ When you gain your fourth or more exhaustion counter, lose half your life, rounded up.
5+ Creatures you control can't attack or block.
6+ You lose the game.
At the beginning of your end step, if you haven't cast a spell or activated an ability this turn, remove an exhaustion counter. Otherwise, put an exhaustion counter on you.
cards.flavor_text: She stopped to rest, just for a moment. She never moved again.

cards.name: The Mine
cards.rules_text:
When The Mine enters, roll 2d6 (gems), then roll 1d10 (ore). Create X Treasure tokens, where X is (gems x 5) plus (ore x 3).
Excavate — At the beginning of your upkeep, roll a d6. Create that many Treasure tokens.
Prospect — {T}, Sacrifice The Mine: Roll 2d6. Create twice that many Treasure tokens.
cards.flavor_text: Some veins run deeper than the earth itself.

cards.name: The Monstrosity
cards.rules_text:
When The Monstrosity enters, roll a d20. Its base power and toughness each become the result of the roll plus 5. An opponent chosen at random gains control of it.
Haste, trample, menace
The Monstrosity can't block.
The Monstrosity attacks the player who drew it each combat if able until that player has 0 or less life or The Monstrosity leaves. When either condition is met, shuffle The Monstrosity into the Deck of Many Things.
cards.flavor_text: Some horrors need no explanation—only survival.

cards.name: Gelatinous Cube
cards.rules_text:
Trample, menace, protection from instants and sorceries
When Gelatinous Cube enters, exile target creature you control. You become engulfed. (Engulfed: Lose 2 life at the beginning of your upkeep. Discard a card and mill three during your draw step. A player can't be engulfed more than once.)
Whenever Gelatinous Cube deals combat damage to a player, that player becomes engulfed. It gets +2/+2 for each engulfed player and attacks each combat if able.
Whenever Gelatinous Cube deals combat damage to a creature, exile it. Put a +1/+1 counter on Gelatinous Cube for each creature exiled with it.
When Gelatinous Cube leaves, all players cease to be engulfed and all creatures exiled with it return to the battlefield.
cards.flavor_text: Its translucent form belies a hunger older than stone.

cards.name: The Path
cards.rules_text:
Creatures you control have haste.
{T}: Add one mana of any color .
Whenever a land enters tapped under your control, untap it.
You may play an additional land on each of your turns.
Landfall — Draw a card.
cards.flavor_text: The road unrolled before her like a welcome mat for the multiverse.

cards.name: The Pit
cards.rules_text:
Roll 3d6. Multiply the result by 3. You lose that much life.
Creatures you control without flying fall — The Pit deals damage to each of them equal to half the life you lost this way, rounded down. Creatures that die this way are exiled instead of going to a graveyard.
If this would reduce your life total to 0 or less, instead your life total becomes 1 and you skip your next two turns.
cards.flavor_text: The ground beneath your feet was never as solid as you believed.

cards.name: The Plant
cards.rules_text:
When The Plant enters, search your library for a land and put it onto the battlefield tapped. Create a 0/1 green Plant token for each land you control.
{T}: Add {G}{G}{G}.
Landfall — Whenever a land you control enters, put a +1/+1 counter on each Plant you control.
{1}: Choose one. The Plant doesn't untap during its controller's next untap step.
• Overgrowth — Create X 0/1 green Plant tokens, where X is the number of lands you control.
• Verdant — Until end of turn, lands you control become 2/2 Forest creatures that are still lands with "Whenever this deals combat damage to a player, create a 0/1 green Plant token."
• Entangle — Target land becomes a 0/4 Plant creature with defender and "{T}: Tap or untap target creature." It's still a land.
cards.flavor_text: First a seed. Then a sapling. Then a forest that remembers your name.

cards.name: The Priest
cards.rules_text:
(Gain the next level as a sorcery to add its ability.)
At the beginning of your upkeep, scry X and gain X life, where X is The Priest's level.
At the beginning of your end step, if you gained life this turn, draw a card.
Level 2 — {2}: Wisdom
Creatures you control have lifelink.
Whenever you gain life, put a +1/+1 counter on target creature you control.
Level 3 — {3}: Wisdom
Whenever a creature enters, you gain 1 life.
Whenever you gain life, you may pay {1}. If you do, create a 1/1 white Spirit token with lifelink.
Level 4 — {4}: Wisdom
At the beginning of your end step, return target creature card with mana value X or less from any graveyard to the battlefield under your control, where X is the life you gained this turn.
Whenever a creature enters under your control, you gain life equal to its toughness.

cards.name: Curse of Restraint
cards.rules_text:
Enchant player, shroud
When Curse of Restraint enters, roll a d6 and add 2. Put that many bind counters on it. You become restrained. (You can't cast more than one spell or activate more than one ability each turn. Artifacts and enchantments you control lose all abilities. Spells you cast cost {1} more for each bind counter on Curse of Restraint. Your creatures can't attack unless you pay {2} per attacker.)
At the beginning of your upkeep, you lose life equal to the number of bind counters on Curse of Restraint.
Whenever a creature deals combat damage to you or a creature you control, it deals 2 additional damage.
{0}, Pay X life and exile X cards from your hand or X nontoken permanents you control: Remove X bind counters from Curse of Restraint. X can't be greater than 2. Activate only as a sorcery.
When Curse of Restraint has no bind counters, sacrifice it.
Eternal Damnation — You can't draw from the Deck of Many Things for the rest of the game.
cards.flavor_text: The chains tightened with every thought of escape.

cards.name: The Ring
cards.rules_text:
Indestructible
When The Ring enters, roll a d12 and add 4. Put that many charge counters on it. You gain protection from everything until your next turn.
At the beginning of your upkeep, put two charge counters on The Ring.
At the beginning of your end step, you lose 1 life for each charge counter on The Ring.
{T}, Remove X charge counters: Choose one —
• Empower — Until end of turn, creatures you control get +X/+X and gain your choice of flying, vigilance, or trample.
• Burden — Draw X cards and lose X life.
• Corrupt — Target player loses X life and you gain X life. X can't be greater than 10.
cards.flavor_text: Some gifts are too powerful to resist, too precious to relinquish.

cards.name: The Shield
cards.rules_text:
Indestructible
Living Weapon (When this Equipment enters, create a 0/0 black Phyrexian Germ token, then attach this Equipment to it.)
Bulwark — At the beginning of your upkeep or whenever equipped creature blocks or becomes blocked, you may put a fortification counter on The Shield.
Equipped creature gets +1/+3 for each fortification counter on The Shield.
Transmutation — {0}: Remove all abilities granted by The Shield from equipped creature. Choose two — Indestructible, Hexproof, Shroud, Protection from a color of your choice, Ward {6}, Absorb 3, Reach, Vigilance, Defender, Persist, Undying, Lifelink, or Regenerate. It gains the chosen abilities until end of turn.
Equip {0}
cards.flavor_text: Those who seek to break it teach it only new ways to be unbreakable.

cards.name: The Ship
cards.rules_text:
At the beginning of your upkeep, create a 2/2 blue, black, and red Pirate token with "When this enters, if you control three or more Pirates, search your library for a land, put it onto the battlefield tapped, then shuffle."
Whenever a Pirate you control attacks, it gains your choice of first strike, deathtouch, lifelink, or vigilance until end of turn.
Whenever a Pirate you control deals combat damage to a player, draw a card and create a Treasure token.
Crew 8
Whenever The Ship becomes crewed, choose three — flying, trample, lifelink, double strike, menace, hexproof, or annihilator 1. The Ship gains those abilities until end of turn.
cards.flavor_text: Part of the crew, part of the ship.

cards.name: The Staff
cards.rules_text:
At the beginning of your upkeep, put a dominion counter on The Staff.
{T}, Choose one. If The Staff has three or more dominion counters, you may choose any number instead:
• Add three mana of any one color.
• Draw two cards, then discard a card.
• Target player loses 3 life and you gain 3 life.
• The Staff deals 3 damage to any target.
• Create a 3/3 blue Elemental token with flying.
• Return target card with mana value 3 or less from your graveyard to your hand.
• Counter target spell or ability.
• Exile target nonland permanent until The Staff leaves.
cards.flavor_text: In its crystalline core, reality itself bends to your will.

cards.name: The Stairway
cards.rules_text:
Choose one —
• Compel — Target player draws a card from the Deck of Many Things.
• Dictate — The next time a player would draw from the Deck of Many Things this game, they draw two cards from it instead and you choose which resolves first. Exile the other.
• Deny — The next time target player would draw a card from the Deck of Many Things this game, that player skips that draw instead.
• Wonder — Search your library for an artifact card and put it onto the battlefield. That artifact gains indestructible. Then shuffle your library.
cards.flavor_text: Some stairs lead up. Some lead down. These lead everywhere at once.

cards.name: Curse of Petrification
cards.rules_text:
Enchant player, shroud
When Curse of Petrification enters, you become petrified. (You can't cast spells or activate abilities except those of Curse of Petrification. Your creatures have indestructible, enter tapped, don't untap, and can't attack. All damage dealt to you or your permanents is reduced to 1. You can't receive poison counters.)
Whenever you're dealt damage, put an erosion counter on Curse of Petrification.
{1}, Pay X life or sacrifice X nontoken, nonland permanents: Put X erosion counters on Curse of Petrification. X can't be greater than 3. Activate only as a sorcery.
When Curse of Petrification has seven or more erosion counters, sacrifice it.
cards.flavor_text: In stillness, eternity. In silence, preservation. In stone, waiting.

cards.name: The Tavern
cards.rules_text:
(Gain the next level as a sorcery to add its ability.)
At the beginning of your upkeep, create X 0/1 black and white Follower tokens with battle cry, where X is The Tavern's level.
Creatures you control have training.
Level 2 — {2}: Charisma
Creatures you control have "Whenever this creature becomes trained, draw a card" and "Tap three untapped creatures you control: Tap target creature an opponent controls."
Level 3 — {3}: Charisma
At the beginning of your upkeep, gain control of target creature an opponent controls with power X or less until end of turn, where X is the number of creatures you control. It gains haste.
Whenever a creature enters under your control, put a +1/+1 counter on it.
Level 4 — {4}: Charisma
Whenever a creature enters under an opponent's control, it enters under your control instead.

cards.name: Divine Intervention
cards.rules_text:
When Divine Intervention enters, exile it. You may cast it from exile without paying its mana cost any time you could cast an instant. If you do, choose a mode you haven't chosen this game:
• Return all permanent cards from your graveyard to the battlefield.
• Your life total becomes your starting life total.
• Exile all nonland permanents your opponents control.
• Search your library for up to five cards and put them into your hand.
If you would lose the game while Divine Intervention is in exile, you may cast it instead. If you do, your life total becomes your starting life total, remove all poison counters from you, reset all commander damage dealt to you, and return target creature from your graveyard to the battlefield tapped.
When Divine Intervention is cast from exile, shuffle it into the Deck of Many Things.
Divine Intervention enters with two intervention counters on it. When you cast Divine Intervention from exile, remove an intervention counter. When the last is removed, exile Divine Intervention permanently.
cards.flavor_text: Pray not for salvation. Pray for timing.

cards.name: True Resurrection
cards.rules_text:
When True Resurrection enters, exile it.
As long as True Resurrection is exiled, if you would lose the game, instead your life total becomes your starting life total, remove all poison counters, curses, and -1/-1 counters from you and permanents you control, and return all creature cards from your graveyard to your hand. You skip your next draw step. Then shuffle True Resurrection into the Deck of Many Things.
You may cast True Resurrection from exile without paying its mana cost any time you could cast an instant. If you do, return target creature card from any graveyard to the battlefield under its owner's control with indestructible until end of turn. Restore all Auras and Equipment that were attached to it when it died. Then shuffle True Resurrection into the Deck of Many Things.
cards.flavor_text: The grave opened. The light poured in. She blinked.

cards.name: The Tower
cards.rules_text:
Collapse — Destroy all artifacts you control. Sacrifice all lands you control. You can’t play lands during your next two turns.
You may draw a card from the Deck of Many Things.
cards.flavor_text: Everything you built. Everything you owned. Rubble.

cards.name: The Tree
cards.rules_text:
Enchant land
Living Growth (When this enters, if you control no lands, create a basic land token of any type, then attach this to it.)
When The Tree enters, draw three cards and gain 8 life.
Enchanted land is a 4/8 green Plant creature with "At the beginning of your upkeep, put two +1/+1 counters on this and create a 0/1 green Plant token with reach and defender." It's still a land.
Other Plants you control get +1/+1 for each counter on enchanted creature.
When The Tree leaves, create X 1/1 green Plant tokens with reach and defender, where X is enchanted creature's toughness. Create a basic Forest land token.
cards.flavor_text: Some seek to escape death. Others choose to become life itself.

cards.name: Vengeful Revenant
cards.rules_text:
Vanishing 10 (This creature enters with ten time counters. At the beginning of your upkeep, remove a time counter. When the last is removed, shuffle it into the Deck of Many Things.)
Haste, flying, shadow
Vengeful Revenant's power and toughness are each equal to 2 plus the number of creature cards in all graveyards.
When Vengeful Revenant enters, if no player is haunted by it, the player who drew it becomes haunted. That player gets an emblem with "At the beginning of your upkeep, sacrifice a creature. If you can't, you lose 1 life." An opponent chosen at random gains control of Vengeful Revenant.
Vengeful Revenant can't block and attacks the haunted player each combat if able.
If Vengeful Revenant would leave the battlefield, return it at the beginning of the next end step instead.
Any player may pay life equal to Vengeful Revenant's power: Shuffle Vengeful Revenant into the Deck of Many Things. Activate only if Vengeful Revenant is on the battlefield.
cards.flavor_text: Born from the slaughter of an entire army, it bears the wrath of a hundred souls.

cards.name: The Warrior
cards.rules_text:
(Gain the next level as a sorcery to add its ability.)
Creatures you control get +X/+X, frenzy X, and rampage X, where X is The Warrior's level.
Whenever one or more creatures you control fight, block, or become blocked, draw a card.
Level 2 — {2}: Strength
Creatures you control have trample and riot.
At the beginning of combat on your turn, target creature you control may fight target creature an opponent controls.
Level 3 — {3}: Strength
Creatures you control have menace and deathtouch.
Whenever a creature you control deals combat damage to a player, put two +1/+1 counters on it.
Level 4 — {4}: Strength
Creatures you control have reach and can't be prevented from dealing combat damage. Each creature you control can block an additional creature and counts as two blockers.
If a creature you control would deal combat damage, it deals double that damage instead.

cards.name: Well of Minor Magics
cards.rules_text:
When Well of Minor Magics enters, choose three from among the following. You gain those abilities for as long as you control Well of Minor Magics.
• At the beginning of your upkeep, scry 1.
• {T}: Add one mana of any color.
• {0}: Target creature you control gains haste until end of turn. Activate only once each turn.
• Whenever you cast a spell, you may pay {1}. If you do, Well of Minor Magics deals 1 damage to any target.
• At the beginning of your end step, draw a card.
cards.flavor_text: Each ripple in its depths whispers secrets of a different art.

cards.name: The Avalanche
cards.rules_text:
Split Second (As long as this spell is on the stack, players can't cast spells or activate abilities that aren't mana abilities.)
Each player sacrifices X lands, where X is the number of lands they control minus 1.
All creatures get -8/-8 until end of turn and can't block this turn. If a creature would die this turn, exile it instead. At the beginning of the next end step, for each creature exiled this way, create a 0/1 colorless Wall token with defender and "When this creature dies, each player loses 1 life."
cards.flavor_text: "The earth doesn't care if you survive. The void beneath it hopes you don't."

cards.name: The Bear
cards.rules_text:
Trample
When The Bear enters, put X +1/+1 counters on it where X is your devotion to green. (Each {G} in the mana costs of permanents you control counts toward your devotion to green.)
Whenever The Bear deals combat damage to a player or permanent, double the number of +1/+1 counters on it.
Pay 2 life: Put a +1/+1 counter on The Bear. Activate only during your turn.
cards.flavor_text: The curse took his form. The blessing took everything else.

cards.name: Curse of Vulnerability
cards.rules_text:
Enchant player
Vanishing 5 (This permanent enters with five time counters on it. At the beginning of your upkeep, remove a time counter from it. When the last is removed, sacrifice it.)
Whenever a creature enchanted player controls blocks or becomes blocked by a creature an opponent controls, that creature gets -2/-2 until end of combat.
If damage would be dealt to enchanted player or creatures they control by a source an opponent controls, that damage is doubled.
cards.flavor_text: "In the end, it wasn't the pain that broke him. It was knowing there would always be more."

cards.name: The Betrayal
cards.rules_text:
Choose target opponent. Gain control of all creatures that player controls until end of turn and they gain haste. For each creature that changed control this way, create a Treachery token. (It's an enchantment token with "At the beginning of your upkeep, the chosen opponent loses 1 life." and "Vanishing 3".)
Whenever a creature you control that you don't own deals combat damage to a player this turn, exile the top card of that player's library. You may play that card this turn without paying its mana cost.
cards.flavor_text: "They fought beside you yesterday. They'll fight against you now." -- Kaya, the Betrayer

cards.name: The Big Sky
cards.rules_text: You get an emblem with "Once during each of your turns, you may have target creature you control get +10/+10 and gain trample until end of turn. When that creature deals combat damage this turn, destroy target artifact."
cards.flavor_text: When the sky opens, even mountains bow.

cards.name: The Brass Dwarf
cards.rules_text:
When The Brass Dwarf enters, create three Forge tokens. (They're artifacts with "{T}, Sacrifice this artifact: Create a token that's a copy of target artifact you control except it's also a 2/2 red and white Construct artifact creature with haste.")
Artifacts you control have "{T}: Add {R}."
Whenever you sacrifice an artifact, The Brass Dwarf deals 2 damage to any target. If the sacrificed artifact was a token, create a Treasure token.
cards.flavor_text: "In my forge, even scrap metal dreams of becoming masterworks."

cards.name: The Carnival
cards.rules_text:
When you cast The Carnival, exile the top nine cards of the Deck of Many Things. An opponent looks at those cards, turns them face down, and shuffles them. Choose one at random, reveal it, then cast it without paying its mana cost. Put the remaining exiled cards into the Deck of Many Things and shuffle.
Flip a coin. If you win the flip, repeat the above process. If you lose the flip, discard a card at random.
cards.flavor_text: "Behind these curtains lie nine fates. Choose carefully — or don't. The show must go on."

cards.name: The Courtesan
cards.rules_text:
When The Courtesan enters, choose target artifact you control. It becomes an Artifact Creature with base power and toughness equal to its mana value and gains indestructible and two of the following at random:
• Flying and vigilance
• Deathtouch and lifelink
• Double strike
• Hexproof and ward {2}
• "Whenever this creature deals combat damage to a player, draw two cards."
• "At the beginning of your upkeep, create a Treasure token."
If you control no artifacts, search your library for an artifact card, put it onto the battlefield, then shuffle. It becomes a 4/4 Artifact Creature with two of the abilities above at random.
When The Courtesan leaves, the chosen artifact remains a creature.
cards.flavor_text: She spoke to the sword, and the sword answered.

cards.name: The Cricket
cards.rules_text:
When The Cricket enters, you may choose to draw up to three additional cards from the Deck of Many Things. For each card drawn this way, put a Cricket counter on The Cricket.
At the beginning of your upkeep, you may reveal the top X cards of your library, where X is the number of Cricket counters on The Cricket. Put any number of lands revealed this way onto the battlefield tapped, then put the rest on the bottom of your library in a random order.
Landfall — Draw X cards for each Cricket counter on The Cricket. This ability triggers only twice each turn.
You may play an additional land on each of your turns for each Cricket counter on The Cricket.
cards.flavor_text: "Curiosity often leaps faster than caution."

cards.name: The Crows
cards.rules_text: Each player chooses a creature they control and an artifact or enchantment they control. An opponent chosen at random chooses one of those permanents for each player. Each player sacrifices the chosen permanent. For each permanent sacrificed this way, that permanent's controller's opponents each draw a card.
cards.flavor_text: "The crows care not for gold or flesh — only that a price is paid."

cards.name: Elder Air Elemental
cards.rules_text:
Vanishing 4 (This permanent enters with four time counters on it. At the beginning of your upkeep, remove a time counter from it. When the last is removed, sacrifice this and exile all of the marked player's nonland permanents. That player planeswalks to the Plane of Air.)
Flash, flying, haste, ward {2}
When The Cyclone enters, mark the player who summoned it, and an opponent chosen at random gains control of it.
The Cyclone attacks the marked player each combat if able and can't block. As long as The Cyclone is on the battlefield, other creatures and players can't attack or target the marked player.
cards.flavor_text: "Face me alone, mortal. Time grows short, and the winds of fate grow restless."

cards.name: The Dance
cards.rules_text:
Initiative — If you would roll a die or flip a coin, instead roll two dice or flip two coins and choose one of those results.
You may cast spells as though they had flash.
Whenever you cast a spell during another player's turn, draw a card.
cards.flavor_text: "To master chance is to learn its rhythm, to feel its pulse, to know its steps."

cards.name: The Demon's Lantern
cards.rules_text:
At the beginning of each opponent's upkeep, that player loses 1 life unless they pay {B}.
{1}: Choose target creature an opponent controls. That creature's controller may pay {1}. If they don't, exile that creature and create a Soul Gem artifact token with:
"When this artifact enters, it gains a charge counter for each point of power the exiled creature had.",
"Remove a charge counter: Add one mana of any color.", and
"{B}, Pay X life, Sacrifice this artifact: Return the exiled creature to the battlefield under its owner's control, where X is that creature's mana value."
If The Demon's Lantern leaves, destroy all Soul Gems.
cards.flavor_text: "The gem's light glows brighter with every whisper of despair."

cards.name: The Desert
cards.rules_text:
Vanishing 3
Caravan — When The Desert enters, search your library for up to three land cards, put them onto the battlefield tapped, then shuffle.
You may exile any number of permanents you control and up to three creatures other players control. When The Desert leaves, return all cards exiled with it to the battlefield under their owners'  control.
cards.flavor_text: One step across burning sand. The next, a thousand leagues away.

cards.name: The Demon
cards.rules_text:
Flying, haste
When The Demon enters, choose a player. That player becomes marked. (A marked player's maximum hand size is reduced by one for each doom counter on The Demon.)
Whenever The Demon deals combat damage to the marked player, put a doom counter on it and scry 1.
At the beginning of marked player's upkeep, The Demon deals damage to that player equal to the number of doom counters on it.
When The Demon has five or more doom counters on it, the marked player loses the game.
cards.flavor_text: "Your soul caught my eye. Soon, it shall catch my claws."

cards.name: The Foreign Trader
cards.rules_text:
Flash, deathtouch
When The Foreign Trader enters, you may pay any amount of life in multiples of 5. For each 5 life paid this way, create three Treasure tokens, put an age counter on it, and scry 1. If you don't, sacrifice it and create a Treasure token.
At the beginning of your upkeep, for each age counter on The Foreign Trader, mill three cards.
Your maximum hand size is increased by the number of age counters on The Foreign Trader and spells you cast cost {1} less for each age counter on it.
Whenever you sacrifice a Treasure token, each opponent loses 1 life and you gain 1 life.
cards.flavor_text: "Time is the only currency that cannot be counterfeited. Shall we trade?"

cards.name: The Forge
cards.rules_text:
When The Forge enters, create three Ember tokens. (Artifacts with "{T}, Sacrifice this: Add {R}{R}.")
{T}, Sacrifice an Equipment you control: Search your library for an Equipment with equal or lesser mana value and put it onto the battlefield attached to a creature you control. It gains "Equipped creature gets +4/+4" and can't be unequipped. Then create an Ember token and shuffle.
Whenever an Equipment enters under your control, create a 3/1 red and white Artificer token with haste and "When this dies, attach target Equipment you control to target creature you control."
Whenever you sacrifice an artifact, The Forge deals 2 damage to any target. If it was a token, The Forge deals 2 additional damage.
cards.flavor_text: She fed the forge a dagger. It returned a destiny.

cards.name: The Eclipse
cards.rules_text:
When The Eclipse enters, each player sacrifices all creatures they control and loses 1 life for each creature sacrificed this way.
Whenever a player loses life, put that many tribute counters on The Eclipse. When it has forty or more, sacrifice it.
At the beginning of each player's upkeep, that player sacrifices a nonland permanent. If they can't, they lose 3 life and discard a card. For each permanent sacrificed, its controller creates a 3/1 red Horror token with "When this leaves, it deals 3 damage to you."
Creatures have "At the beginning of your end step, exile this unless you pay life equal to its power."
cards.flavor_text: "All things end. The Eclipse demands tribute."

cards.name: The Empty Throne
cards.rules_text:
When The Empty Throne enters, create five Treasure tokens.
Decree — {T}, Choose one —
• Prosperity — Create two Treasure tokens and gain 2 life.
• Nobility — Create two 1/1 white and black Noble tokens with "Other creatures you control get +1/+0."
• Ascension — Nobles you control gain flying and vigilance until the end of your next turn.
• Tribute — Each opponent loses life equal to the number of Nobles you control.
Nobles you control have "Whenever this creature attacks, create a Treasure token."
cards.flavor_text: The throne sat empty for a thousand years. It was waiting for you.

cards.name: The Hidden Truth
cards.rules_text: Search any player's library, hand, or graveyard. You may put a permanent card found this way onto the battlefield under your control or cast a nonpermanent card found this way without paying its mana cost. If you searched a library, its owner shuffles.
cards.flavor_text: Ask anything. The answer will haunt you.

cards.name: The Idiot
cards.rules_text:
Enchant player
The Idiot has hexproof from enchanted player.
Mindless — Enchanted player casts spells and activates abilities without choosing targets or modes. When a spell or ability that player controls would require a target or choice, choose it at random from the legal options.
Drooling — At the beginning of enchanted player's upkeep, that player mills two cards, then discards a card at random.
Blank Slate — Enchanted player can't look at the top card of their library or any face-down cards, and loses 1 life whenever they draw a card.
cards.flavor_text: "Once-sharp thoughts now scatter like leaves in a storm, never to be gathered again."

cards.name: The Inquisitor
cards.rules_text:
Flash
When The Inquisitor enters, look at target opponent's hand. You may exile a card from it. Until the end of your next turn, that player can't cast spells that share a card type with the exiled card.
{T}, {2}, Sacrifice The Inquisitor: Look at target opponent's hand and choose up to three cards from it. That player exiles those cards. Until end of turn, you may cast one of the exiled cards without paying its mana cost. At the beginning of the next end step, return the other cards to their owner's hand.
cards.flavor_text: "Your secrets are but bargaining chips in our little game of truth."

cards.name: The Joke
cards.rules_text:
Choose another player. That player is your ally until end of turn.
Exile the top three cards of the Deck of Many Things face up. Your ally chooses one of those cards and casts it without paying its mana cost. You choose one of the remaining two cards and cast it without paying its mana cost. Exile the third card.
cards.flavor_text: "Three fates revealed, two embraced, one forever lost — even destiny has a sense of humor."

cards.name: The Juggler
cards.rules_text:
Flash, haste
When The Juggler enters, exile the top three cards of your library face up. For as long as they remain exiled, you may cast spells from among them, and you may spend mana as though it were mana of any color to cast them.
At the beginning of your upkeep, you may return any number of exiled cards you own to your library, then exile that many cards from the top of your library face up.
Whenever you cast a spell from exile, copy it and create a token copy of The Juggler. You may choose new targets for the copy.
cards.flavor_text: "Reality is merely another ball to keep in the air."

cards.name: The Keep
cards.rules_text:
When The Keep enters, draw two cards, create two Treasure tokens, then exile any number of nonland permanents you control. Those cards gain "You may cast this card from exile."
At the beginning of your upkeep, you may return up to two cards exiled with The Keep to the battlefield under your control.
{2}, {T}: Exile target permanent you control. It gains "You may cast this card from exile."
cards.flavor_text: A fortress between worlds, where even time asks permission to enter.

cards.name: The Liar
cards.rules_text:
Flash
Cursed Possession — When The Liar enters, choose target artifact or equipment an opponent controls with the highest mana value. Put a curse counter on it.
Permanents with curse counters have "At the beginning of your upkeep, this permanent deals 3 damage to you" and "Activated abilities of this permanent cost {3} more to activate."
Whenever a permanent with a curse counter on it is sacrificed or destroyed, The Liar's controller creates a Treasure token and draws a card.
{T}: Put a curse counter on target artifact.
cards.flavor_text: "Truth bends like light through crystal — and I am the one who holds the prism."

cards.name: The Locksmith
cards.rules_text:
Flash
When The Locksmith enters, choose one —
• Unlock — Until your next turn, target permanent loses all abilities and can't be sacrificed.
• Duplicate — Create a colorless Key artifact token with "{T}, Sacrifice this: Target permanent can't attack, block, or activate abilities until end of turn."
{1}, {T}: Put a key counter on target artifact, creature, or enchantment. (Permanents with key counters have "{T}: Add one mana of any color.")
{5}, {T}, Remove all key counters from permanents you control: Gain control of target permanent with mana value X or less, where X is the number of counters removed. Activate only as a sorcery and only once per game.
cards.flavor_text: There is no door I cannot open. That is both my gift and your warning.

cards.name: The Lost
cards.rules_text:
All non-Spirit creatures lose all abilities and can't gain abilities. They can't transform or have counters put on them.
If a non-Spirit creature would die, exile it instead. That creature's controller creates a 1/1 black and green Spirit token with "This creature's power and toughness are each equal to the number of Spirits you control."
Whenever a Spirit enters, its controller gains 1 life and each other player loses 1 life.
Whenever a non-Spirit creature enters, its controller loses 2 life and each other player gains 2 life.
cards.flavor_text: "Some paths, once abandoned, lead only to stranger roads."

cards.name: The Marriage
cards.rules_text:
When The Marriage enters, target player gains control of target nonland permanent you control, then you gain control of target nonland permanent they control. Put a blessing counter on each.
Permanents with blessing counters have hexproof, vigilance, and "At the beginning of your upkeep, create a 1/1 white and green Spirit token with 'Whenever this attacks, put a blessing counter on another target permanent you control.'"
At the beginning of each end step, if you control four or more permanents with blessing counters, you may remove all blessing counters from permanents you control. If you do, search your library for any number of permanent cards with total mana value X or less, where X is twice the counters removed, and put them onto the battlefield with blessing counters.
cards.flavor_text: The vows were spoken between worlds, and the multiverse bore witness.

cards.name: The Midwife
cards.rules_text:
Flash
Whenever a permanent not named The Midwife enters under your control, put a birth counter on it.
When The Midwife enters, create two 1/1 white green Spirit tokens with lifelink.
Permanents you control with birth counters on them have "At the beginning of your upkeep, create a non-legendary token that's a copy of this permanent."
cards.flavor_text: Where she walks, nothing remains barren.

cards.name: The Mountain Man
cards.rules_text:
Haste, trample, ward {2}
When The Mountain Man enters or attacks, you may search your library for up to two land cards, put them onto the battlefield tapped, then shuffle.
The Mountain Man's power and toughness are each equal to twice the number of lands you control, rounded down.
Landfall — The Mountain Man deals damage equal to half the number of lands you control, rounded down, to any target.
cards.flavor_text: "The peaks do not yield to those who climb them. They yield to those who become them."

cards.name: The Mute Hag
cards.rules_text:
Shroud, indestructible
When The Mute Hag enters, each opponent gets a deprivation counter of a random type. The types are:
• Sight — Can't scry, surveil, or search libraries. Spells and abilities you control can't target opponents' permanents.
• Hearing — Can't cast spells or activate abilities during other players' turns.
• Speech — Creatures you control can't attack or block.
At the beginning of each opponent's upkeep, if that player has fewer than three deprivation counters, they get one of a random type they don't already have.
Deprivation counters can't be removed except by an effect from a card named The Fates.
cards.flavor_text: "In the absence of one sense, terror fills all others."

cards.name: The Owl
cards.rules_text:
Flash
Flying, ward {2}
Whenever you scry, you may pay {1}. If you do, gain 2 life and draw a card.
At the beginning of your upkeep, scry 1.
{T}, Sacrifice The Owl: Scry 7, then target opponent may scry 3. Draw a card.
When The Owl dies, investigate. (Create a Clue token. It's an artifact with "{2}, Sacrifice this artifact: Draw a card.")
cards.flavor_text: It sees what you see. It sees what they see. It sees what no one sees.

cards.name: The Paladin
cards.rules_text:
Flying, double strike, vigilance
When The Paladin enters, choose one —
• You create five 2/2 white Knight tokens with vigilance.
• You gain 10 life and draw three cards.
• You get an emblem with "Creatures you control have protection from the color of your choice and lifelink."
Whenever The Paladin attacks, exile target nonland permanent an opponent controls until The Paladin leaves.
At the beginning of your end step, return target permanent card from your graveyard to the battlefield.
cards.flavor_text: She arrived without being summoned. Evil had that effect on her.

cards.name: The Peacock
cards.rules_text:
When The Peacock enters, creatures you control gain indestructible until end of turn.
Creatures you control get +0/+4 and have hexproof.
Creatures can't attack you unless their controller pays {2} for each creature they control that's attacking you.
Creatures you control get -1/+0.
cards.flavor_text: Invulnerable, immaculate, immovable.

cards.name: The Publican
cards.rules_text:
When The Publican enters, create a Tavern artifact token.
Tavern tokens have "At the beginning of each player's upkeep, that player may pay {1} and 1 life. If they do, they draw a card and create a 1/1 white and black Human Patron token."
Whenever a player creates a Patron token, each player may pay {1}. Each player who does creates a Treasure token and gains 1 life.
At the beginning of each end step, any player who controls three or more Patron tokens may exile those tokens. When they do, that player gains control of target nonland permanent and creates a Tavern token.
cards.flavor_text: "A round of drinks opens more doors than a battering ram."

cards.name: The Queen Mother
cards.rules_text:
When The Queen Mother enters, create X 1/2 green and black Ant tokens with reach and vigilance, where X is 2 plus the result of rolling two four-sided dice.
Ants you control have "Whenever this creature blocks or becomes blocked, put a +1/+1 counter on it."
The Queen Mother has indestructible and hexproof as long as you control an Ant.
Whenever The Queen Mother becomes the target of a spell or ability an opponent controls, create a 2/3 green and black Ant token with reach and vigilance.
cards.flavor_text: She speaks, and ten thousand legs answer.

cards.name: The Rabbit Prince
cards.rules_text:
Double strike, haste
If a creature would deal combat damage to any target, it deals double that damage instead.
Whenever The Rabbit Prince deals combat damage to a player for the first time each turn, you may have target creature you control gain double strike and haste until end of turn. That creature gets +X/+0 until end of turn, where X is the amount of combat damage The Rabbit Prince dealt to that player this turn. Then untap all creatures you control. After this phase, there is an additional combat phase.
cards.flavor_text: "In the Court of Swift Justice, every strike tells two tales."— The Rabbit Prince

cards.name: The Rakshasa
cards.rules_text:
Flying, ward {2}
Curse of Deception — Whenever a player casts a spell that targets only a single creature or player, roll a d6. On a 1 or 2, that spell's controller transforms their most powerful creature into a 1/1 green Frog token until end of turn. (It loses all abilities.)
Whenever a creature becomes a Frog this way, The Rakshasa's controller draws a card.
When The Rakshasa leaves, shuffle it into the Deck of Many Things.
cards.flavor_text: Every deception carries a price. The Rakshasa simply collects.

cards.name: Curse of Leprosy
cards.rules_text:
Enchant player
When Curse of Leprosy enters and during your upkeep, put a disease counter on it.
At the beginning of your end step, lose 2 life for each disease counter on Curse of Leprosy.
Creatures you control can't gain counters and get -1/-1 for each disease counter on Curse of Leprosy.
Pay X life, where X is twice the number of disease counters on Curse of Leprosy: Remove a disease counter. Activate only as a sorcery and only if Curse of Leprosy has two or more disease counters on it.
cards.flavor_text: "The flesh remembers what the mind wishes to forget."

cards.name: The Snakebite
cards.rules_text:
Split second (As long as this spell is on the stack, players can't cast spells or activate abilities that aren't mana abilities.)
Put X -1/-1 counters on target creature, where X is twice that creature's power. Then create X 1/1 green Snake tokens with deathtouch and wither.
Exile The Snakebite with three time counters on it and suspend 3. (At the beginning of your upkeep, remove a time counter. When the last is removed, cast this card without paying its mana cost.)
When you cast The Snakebite from exile, double the number of poison counters each player has.
cards.flavor_text: "First comes fear. Then pain. Finally, the blessed numbness of acceptance."

cards.name: The Survivor
cards.rules_text: If you would lose the game, instead shuffle your hand, your graveyard, and all permanents you own into your library, then draw seven cards and your life total becomes your starting life total. You may cast three spells from your hand without paying their mana costs.
cards.flavor_text: She died. And then she didn't.

cards.name: The Tangled Briar
cards.rules_text:
{T}: Add one mana of any color.
{T}: Look at the top X cards of your library, where X is the number of lands you control. You may reveal a land card from among them and put it onto the battlefield. If you do, you may cast a creature spell from your hand without paying its mana cost. Then roll a d6. Create a number of 3/3 green Plant creature tokens with trample equal to the result plus two under a random opponent's control. Those creatures must attack you each combat if able.
Landfall — Whenever a land enters under your control, put a +1/+1 counter on target creature you control.
cards.flavor_text: The briar whispers where treasure lies buried. The shambling mounds remember where you dug.

cards.name: The Teamster
cards.rules_text:
When The Teamster enters, choose one —
• Begin a quest. (Create a Quest enchantment token with "At the beginning of your upkeep, you lose 2 life" and "When you cast a spell with mana value 5 or greater, sacrifice this enchantment and search your library for a card, put it into your hand, then shuffle.")
• Accept a burden. (You get an emblem with "At the beginning of your upkeep, you lose 2 life" and "When you cast a spell with mana value 5 or greater, you lose this emblem.")
{1}, Sacrifice The Teamster: Create a 4/4 black and white Avatar token with flying and lifelink, then create two Treasure tokens. Activate only if you don't control a Quest token from The Teamster and don't have an emblem from The Teamster.
cards.flavor_text: The task was impossible. The payment was irresistible.

cards.name: The Theatre
cards.rules_text:
At the beginning of your upkeep, create a 1/1 green and white Human Performer token and put a Spotlight counter on The Theatre.
Whenever a creature dies, you may put an additional Spotlight counter on The Theatre.
{T}, Remove a Spotlight counter from The Theatre: Choose one —
• Encore performance — Create two 2/2 white Human Performer tokens with "When this creature enters, tap target creature an opponent controls."
• Inspire the audience — Each player draws a card, then you draw two additional cards.
• Standing ovation — Each player gains 1 life, then you gain 1 life for each creature you control.
cards.flavor_text: The curtain rises. The audience holds its breath. You are both actor and playwright.

cards.name: The Trumpet
cards.rules_text:
Abilities you activate cost {X} less to activate, where X is half the number of lands you control, rounded down.
{8}, {T}: Create a creature token with power and toughness each equal to X. Choose one —
• A white Knight with first strike and vigilance.
• A black Demon with menace and deathtouch.
• A blue Elemental with flying and lifelink.
{8}, {T}: Search your library for a creature card with mana value less than or equal to X, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then shuffle. You may cast that card this turn without paying its mana cost.
cards.flavor_text: One note. Across every plane, every hero turns toward the sound.

cards.name: The Twin
cards.rules_text:
Flash, haste
{T}: Choose target creature. The Twin becomes a copy of that creature except its power and toughness are swapped, it's untapped if the original is tapped (and vice versa), and +1/+1 counters on it become -1/-1 counters. It gains this ability.
